steps.title: Find the central contrast
steps.content: Critics warned that writing in Gikuyu might limit Kamau's reach, but he went on to write acclaimed works in Gikuyu that were translated into more than thirty languages. The main idea must include both the skepticism and the later success.

steps.title: Keep both halves
steps.content: Choice A incorrectly suggests his first works were in Gikuyu. Choice C misstates the discussion of widely understood languages. Choice D focuses on his decision but omits the successful outcome. The remaining choice captures skepticism that did not prevent literary success. The answer is B.
